channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Yanga Begins New Season Without Key Players Mzize, Job, and Pacome
item.description: The article discusses the Tanzanian football team Yanga's preparations for the new season, highlighting the absence of key players Mzize, Job, and Pacome. It also mentions other sports-related events,...

item.title: Pastor threatens journalist during Govender brothers' murder trial
item.description: The Durban High Court issued a stern warning to the public attending the murder trial of Ferrel and Darren Govender, urging them not to intimidate or threaten journalists covering the proceedings. Sen...

item.title: Chargrill Charlie’s: From Family Run to Corporate Giant, Maintaining Quality Amid Growth
item.description: Chargrill Charlie’s, a Sydney-based chicken takeaway chain founded in 1989 by South African migrants Maon and Saul Sher, has grown from a small family business into a corporate entity under Craveable ...

item.title: Richard Gutierrez's Action Expertise in 'Blood vs Duty' Highlighted Through 10 Key Scenes
item.description: The article discusses 10 standout action sequences from the Philippine TV series 'Blood vs Duty,' focusing on Richard Gutierrez's portrayal of Marcus Reyes. These scenes are designed to showcase his p...

item.title: Global Photobook Festivals Calendar for 2023
item.description: This article compiles a comprehensive list of photobook and ebook-related festivals and events scheduled for 2023, spanning multiple continents and cultural contexts. The calendar highlights over 50 i...

item.title: Presidency Highlights US-Nigeria Security Collaboration Success
item.description: The Nigerian Presidency has praised a letter from US President Donald Trump to President Bola Tinubu, calling it evidence of successful security cooperation between the two nations. The letter, releas...

item.title: Senate Impeachment Trial of VP Sara Duterte Continues with Key Testimonies
item.description: The Senate impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte resumed on July 22, 2026, with National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag returning to the witness stand for cross-exami...

item.title: Natalie Fleet resigns as safeguarding minister after emotional strain
item.description: Natalie Fleet, a Labour MP and survivor of teenage sexual abuse, has resigned as safeguarding minister following the emotional toll of the role. The position, which involves handling cases of child ab...

item.title: Tyre faces heritage risks amid conservation concerns
item.description: The ancient Lebanese city of Tyre, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, has been designated as a 'heritage danger' location due to increasing threats from urban development and environmental degradation. Thi...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Continues Hearings on Justice System Corruption
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry into corruption within the criminal justice system is ongoing, with hearings continuing on Wednesday, July 22, 2026. The commission, which investigates allegations o...

item.title: Punjab Farmers Demand Better Crop Price Support
item.description: Punjab's farmers are protesting against the government's crop price support policy, which they claim fails to address their financial struggles. The standoff has escalated as farmers demand fairer pri...

item.title: Avocados Drive Employment and Economic Growth in Tanzania's Rural Areas
item.description: The article highlights how the avocado industry, dubbed 'green gold,' is transforming rural economies in Tanzania by creating employment opportunities for young people. Once viewed as a subsistence cr...

item.title: Court Grants Defense Access To DSS Director-General In Cybercrime Trial Over Social Media Posts
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has allowed Omoyele Sowore’s defense team to summon the Director-General of the Department of State Services (DSS), Tosin Ajayi, to testify in his ongoing cybercrime tr...

item.title: House Speaker Urges Protection of Food-Producing Provinces for National Food Security
item.description: House Speaker Faustino Dy III has called on the government to prioritize the protection of food-producing provinces to ensure national food security. During a briefing on El Niño preparedness, Dy high...

item.title: Tom Morello Honors Late Mother with Memorial Project Focused on Compassion and Activism
item.description: Tom Morello, guitarist of Rage Against The Machine, has launched the Mary Morello Memorial Project to honor his late mother, who passed away at the age of 102. The initiative encourages people to supp...

item.title: Union minister urges parliamentary debate on exam irregularities amid youth protests
item.description: Union Education Minister JP Nadda called for a parliamentary discussion on alleged examination paper leaks, urging the Opposition not to politicise the issue. He highlighted cases in non-BJP governed ...

item.title: BC Wildfires Close Key Highways, Disrupt Travel Between Lower Mainland and Interior
item.description: Wildfires in British Columbia have forced the closure of two major highways, disrupting travel between the Lower Mainland and the Interior. The Anderson Creek wildfire, located south of Hicks, has gro...

item.title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein dies after alleged crimes and legal scrutiny
item.description: Daniel Siad, a French modelling scout with ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors confirmed. Siad's body was discovered on ...

item.title: GGML Mine Success Highlights Tanzanian Leadership in Mining Sector
item.description: The article discusses Tanzania's significant achievement at the GGML mine, emphasizing the country's growing influence in the mining sector. With 80% of the mine's leadership being Tanzanians, this ma...

item.title: Samsung Unveils New Galaxy Z Series Smartphones with Advanced Features and Competitive Pricing
item.description: Samsung has launched its latest Galaxy Z Series smartphones, featuring foldable designs, enhanced AI capabilities, and improved camera systems. The series includes three models: the Galaxy Fold8 Ultra...
